2604.24559.title: Aligned Multi-View Scripts for Universal Chart-to-Code Generation
2604.24559.paper_summary: Chart-to-code generation converts a chart image into an executable plotting script, enabling faithful reproduction and editable visualizations. Existing methods are largely Python-centric, limiting practical use and overlooking a critical source of supervision: the same chart can be expressed by semantically equivalent scripts in different plotting languages. To fill this gap, we introduce Chart2NCode, a dataset of 176K charts paired with aligned scripts in Python, R, and LaTeX that render visually equivalent outputs, constructed via a metadata-to-template pipeline with rendering verification and human quality checks. Building on a LLaVA-style architecture, we further propose CharLuMA, a parameter-efficient adaptation module that augments the multimodal projector with a language-conditioned mixture of low-rank subspaces, allowing the model to share core chart understanding while specializing code generation to the target language through lightweight routing. Extensive experiments show consistent gains in executability and visual fidelity across all languages, outperforming strong open-source baselines and remaining competitive with proprietary systems. Further analyses reveal that balanced multi-language supervision benefits all languages and that the adapter allocates a compact shared core plus language-specific capacity. Codes and data are available at https://github.com/Zhihan72/CharLuMA.
2604.24559.paper_summary_zh: 圖表到代碼生成將圖表圖像轉換為可執行的繪圖腳本，使得忠實再現和可編輯的視覺化成為可能。現有的方法主要以 Python 為中心，限制了實際應用並忽視了一個關鍵的監督來源：同一圖表可以用不同繪圖語言中的語義等價腳本來表達。為填補這一空白，我們引入了 Chart2NCode，這是一個包含 176K 張圖表及其在 Python、R 和 LaTeX 中對應腳本的數據集，這些腳本產生視覺上等價的輸出，通過元數據到模板的管道構建，並進行渲染驗證和人工質量檢查。在 LLaVA 風格的架構基礎上，我們進一步提出了 CharLuMA，一個參數高效的適應模塊，該模塊通過語言條件的低秩子空間混合來增強多模態投影儀，使模型能夠共享核心圖表理解，同時通過輕量級路由專門化代碼生成到目標語言。大量實驗顯示，在所有語言中可執行性和視覺保真度的一致增益，超越了強大的開源基準，並在與專有系統的競爭中保持競爭力。進一步分析顯示，平衡的多語言監督對所有語言都有益，並且適配器分配了一個緊湊的共享核心加上語言特定的容量。代碼和數據可在 https://github.com/Zhihan72/CharLuMA 獲得。
2604.24559.author: Zhihan Zhang et.al.
